fr:title.text: The category of cpos from a synthetic viewpoint

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The category of cpos from a synthetic viewpoint
fr:frontmatter.fr:meta: MFPS ’97: Thirteenth Annual Conference on Mathematical Foundations of Progamming Semantics

@inproceedings{fiore-rosolini-1997-mfps, author = {Fiore, Marcelo P. and Rosolini, Giuseppe}, editor = {Brookes, Stephen D. and Mislove, Michael W.}, publisher = {Elsevier}, booktitle = {Thirteenth Annual Conference on Mathematical Foundations of Progamming Semantics, {MFPS} 1997, Carnegie Mellon University, Pittsburgh, PA, USA, March 23-26, 1997}, year = {1997}, doi = {10.1016/S1571-0661(05)80165-3}, pages = {133--150}, series = {Electronic Notes on Theoretical Computer Sciecnce}, title = {The category of cpos from a synthetic viewpoint}, volume = {6}, }

fr:title.text: When is the partial map classifier a Sierpiński cone?

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: When is the partial map classifier a Sierpiński cone?

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Interval objects in a category of spaces can act as parameterising shapes for “arrows” drawn in a given space. Riehl and Shulman have shown that in homotopy type theory extended by a linear interval, internal orthogonality laws can be imposed to isolate the types that behave like (higher) categories with respect to the induced notion of arrow, leading to a synthetic theory of higher categories. This idea of isolating internal orthogonality classes of types via an interval has its origin in the synthetic domain theory of the 1990s. The latter has revealed additional axioms on the interval that cause such orthogonal classes to behave more or less like full internal subcategories of predomains in the spirit of Scott.

In this paper, we study the relationship between partial map classifiers, Sierpiński cones, and axioms for synthetic higher category and domain theory within Voevodsky’s univalent foundations. In particular, we show that the Phoa principle ensures that synthetic ∞-categories are closed under partial map classifiers, and we isolate a reflective subuniverse of types within which the Sierpiński cone (a lax colimit) can be computed as a partial map classifier by strengthening the Segal condition.

fr:title.text: Baby steps in higher domain theory › analytic vs. synthetic domain theory (III)

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Baby steps in higher domain theory › analytic vs. synthetic domain theory (III)

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: In the 1990s, Fiore and Rosolini and Fiore and Plotkin studied the representation of analytic categories of domains in toposes of synthetic domains by taking sheaves, building on prior work of Scott.

We hope that we can also take {\mathopen {}\left (\infty ,1\right )\mathclose {}}-sheaves on a dense generator of the category of \omega -cpos to obtain a model of higher synthetic domain theory. Our existing work on lifting should apply.
Open question. We must get a better understanding of models of SDT as classifying toposes in order to continue to synthetic quasicoherence.
Related to our current efforts, we hope also to investigate whether the formally (co)complete cuboidal sets of Fiore, Plotkin, and Power (1997) have a synthetic counterpart.
